structuralIssues.description: 3 content sections empty: standards_development, stakeholder_notes, key_risks, sources_summary.

EN 16510-2-7:2025 harmonized via Implementing Decision (EU) 2026/284 (OJ 9 Feb 2026). Coexistence period with EN 13240:2001 and EN 14785:2006 ends 9 Feb 2027. Stage field may need update to reflect this.
structuralIssues.action: Update stage field for EN 16510-2-7 when applying updates

structuralIssues.title: Triple regulation: CPR + Ecodesign revision + ESPR create dual DPP obligation risk
structuralIssues.description: Space heating appliances face unprecedented regulatory overlay: CPR 2024/3110 (construction performance, EN 16510, future DPP), Ecodesign 2015/1185 (energy efficiency, emissions, revision consultation Dec 2025), and ESPR 2024/1781 (potential future DPP for energy-related products). DPP may be required from both CPR and ESPR tracks. EC has identified discrepancies between CPR and Ecodesign for solid fuel appliances — resolution pending. SHA is one of few families at direct CPR/ESPR intersection.
structuralIssues.source: EC Ecodesign consultation (https://energy.ec.europa.eu/news/ecodesign-and-energy-labelling-commission-consults-revised-rules-space-and-water-heaters-2025-12-01_en); Circularise ESPR DPP sectors (https://www.circularise.com/blogs/dpps-required-by-eu-legislation-across-sectors); Construction Products Europe (https://construction-products.eu/publications/sustainable-products-initiative/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document triple regulation in content.key_risks. Monitor EC resolution of CPR/Ecodesign discrepancies.

structuralIssues.title: CEFACD warns of sector crisis: EN 16510 + Ecodesign revision = dual compliance squeeze
structuralIssues.description: CEFACD (7 Feb 2025) warns that Ecodesign revision could destroy sector representing ~11,000 SMEs and 200,000 jobs, claiming 100% of new solid fuel heaters could disappear. No Notified Bodies in Europe notified for EN 16510 conformity assessment. July 2027 Ecodesign compliance date 'completely unrealistic.' Re-testing entire portfolios for both EN 16510 AND new Ecodesign limits simultaneously is unreasonable. European Parliament question E-000487/2025 raised risk of wood stove ban.
structuralIssues.source: CEFACD position paper (https://cefacd.com/images/CEFACD_Position_Paper_on_Ecodesign_review_070225.pdf); EP question E-000487/2025 (https://www.europarl.europa.eu/doceo/document/E-10-2025-000487_EN.html)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document industry crisis warning in content.key_risks. Monitor Ecodesign revision outcome and Notified Body notification status.

structuralIssues.title: Zero stove EPDs found — industry has no EN 15804 environmental data infrastructure
structuralIssues.description: No Environmental Product Declarations (EPDs) per EN 15804 found for wood stoves, pellet stoves, or any space heating appliance. Industry focuses on Ecodesign energy labelling (A++ to G) and emission certifications (PM/OGC/CO/NOx), not lifecycle environmental declarations. DPP GWP under new CPR requires EN 15804+A2 data. Heating appliance LCA is complex: operational emissions + fuel supply + manufacturing + end-of-life. Fundamentally different from structural product EPDs.
structuralIssues.source: EPD International (https://www.environdec.com/services/what-is-epd); EHI Heating Market Report (https://ehi.eu/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/HMR2024_web.pdf)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document EPD readiness gap in content. Flag as major DPP implementation barrier for heating appliance manufacturers.
